task_goal: Repair App/Core/Harbor runtime admission contract consumption and packaged runtime launch so App can supervise bundled local Core/Harbor runtimes, keep fixture/demo state out of live availability, and retain a visible Electron window for E2E.
subagent_notes: Harbor lane output is integrated in the Harbor worktree and consumed by APP-265 as an external PR/head dependency, not as an App-owned delegation.
Core lane output is integrated in the Core worktree and consumed by APP-265 as an external PR/head dependency, not as an App-owned delegation.

App blocker explorer 019f431f-8315-7541-b8ba-ceb21cee7320 reported that packaged smoke did not prove a stable visible OS window and that App needed packaged start-runtime resources before it could open as an out-of-the-box runtime shell.
App lane code changes are integrated in this worktree and represented by integration_evidence entries below.

external_dependencies.boundary: No App code changed from Harbor lane; Harbor PR #232 is merged and was consumed by local API admission E2E.
No App code changed from Core lane; Core PR #253 is merged and was consumed by local API admission E2E.

integration_evidence.summary: App build now packages sibling Core and Harbor build outputs into dist-electron/runtime with start-runtime.mjs wrappers, then launches them through the Electron Node runtime on the configured local endpoints.
App Harbor supervisor probes readiness-compatible owner endpoints and packaged vertical smoke covers Harbor /readiness-only compatibility.
Core admission remains required for live gate availability; malformed, missing, fixture, demo, or unavailable facts fail closed.
App exposes runtime probe diagnostics so users can see which local owner runtime gate is blocking live operation.

Packaged smoke injects isolated local connection endpoints and uses temporary userData so host 8787/8788 runtime state cannot contaminate fail-closed checks; the runtime smoke leaves packaged runtime enabled to prove bundled Core/Harbor launch.
Normal Electron startup retains BrowserWindow references and explicitly shows/focuses the main window for future Computer Use E2E once the workstation is unlocked.

runtime_boundary.runtime_evidence_kind: local packaged Core/Harbor runtime smoke, owner-shaped smoke, and App runtime gate diagnostics
runtime_boundary.profile: none opened by this App lane

remaining_limitations: Final visible App E2E with Computer Use and real browser provider/session/task flows remains outside APP-265 and must run before closing the broader App #14 user stories.
